data.task:
Phase 1 foundation for ADR-0037 (founder stop control: halt-and-hold). SCHEMA ONLY — do NOT wire the runner loop yet (that is the next atom). Land the durable contract the runtime will consume.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. packages/core/src/store/types.ts — add 'held' to the RunStatus union: `export type RunStatus = 'running' | 'awaiting-founder' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'stopped' | 'held'`. Above the union, write a one-owner doc comment that draws the ADR-0037 disposition distinction verbatim in spirit: held = loop paused mid-flight, resume re-enters at the parked atom (non-terminal, panes open); wrapup = run's work for this launch is complete, resume is a fresh launch; stopped = terminal stopRun() (atom abandoned + quarantined); teardown = a pane/session lifecycle action, never a run disposition. Point the comment at ADR-0037 as the owner.
2. NEW module packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts defining the runner-owned stop-signal contract:
   - The artifact filename the PERSONA writes into the run directory to record an explicit founder stop (e.g. STOP_SIGNAL_FILENAME = 'founder-stop.json'), and a path helper given a run dir.
   - The founder-stop trigger schema: minimally a marker that this records an EXPLICIT FOUNDER direction (e.g. { kind: 'founder-stop', recordedBy: 'oscar'|'bob'|'deb', note?: string }). This is founder-explicit-only — there is NO programmatic persona self-stop function; the artifact only ever represents a recorded founder instruction. Document that invariant in the module header.
   - A reader `readFounderStopSignal(runDir): FounderStopSignal | null` that returns the parsed signal when present and well-formed, and null when absent. Malformed JSON must be a hard error (do not silently treat corrupt as absent — surface it), per the ADR's 'consume the same stop artifact' intent.
   - The resume-state record TYPES the runner will persist at the three registration points from ADR-0037 §Resume-state contract: a discriminated union over park markers 'pre-dispatch' | 'during-exec' | 'pre-verdict', each carrying the fields the ADR names (pre-dispatch: parked directive + atom number; during-exec: active atom number + live directive + wait/monitor cursor handle; pre-verdict: active atom number + the verify request already handed to Oscar). Define the types and a writer/reader pair for this record alongside the stop signal in the run dir. The runtime atom will populate them — here you only define and unit-test the shapes + read/write round-trip.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- New unit test file packages/core/tests/founder-stop.test.ts covering: reader returns null when absent; round-trips a valid founder-stop signal; throws on malformed JSON; resume-state record write→read round-trips for all three park markers; and a test asserting the module exposes no self-stop/auto-stop trigger (founder-explicit-only — e.g. the only way to produce the artifact is recording a founder direction).
- `cd '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder/packages/core' && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test` is GREEN (paste the tail showing the founder-stop suite passing and the exit code).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Do NOT touch stopRun(), teardownRun(), POST /runs/:id/stop, or any teardown/quarantine semantics — the existing terminal 'stopped' path stays exactly as is. This atom adds the 'held' member and the new contract module only; it does not yet route anything to 'held'.
- Do not wire the runner loop, monitor, nudge, or status projection to the new artifact yet.
- Keep the whole core test suite green, not just the new file.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/store/types.ts, packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts (new), packages/core/tests/founder-stop.test.ts (new). One-shot, judgment-gated (artifact/record schema design); not loop-amenable.

data.detail: awaiting-directive atom 1: awaiting directive 1
awaiting-directive atom 1: awaiting Oscar's directive for atom 1

data.task:
Phase 1 runtime, part A for ADR-0037 (founder stop = halt-and-hold). Wire the runner loop to honor a founder-stop artifact AT THE DIRECTIVE (pre-dispatch) BOUNDARY ONLY and park into a new `held` disposition. The during-exec and pre-verdict boundaries are the NEXT atom — do NOT touch executeAgentStep's internals here beyond what's listed.

OWNER MAP (read these in packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts before changing):
- The terminal stop path today: AbortSignal (deps.signal) -> StopRequestedError -> outer catch at ~line 1558: `if (isStopRequestedError(err)) return await stopRun()`. `stopRun()` (~1339) kills bob, marks the work item `abandoned`, quarantines the atom, sets status `stopped`. This is the OPERATOR-terminate path and MUST stay exactly as-is. `held` must NOT reuse it.
- The directive (pre-dispatch) boundary: top of the `for(;;)` loop (~1382-1390), `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog('directive', n, ...)` -> `io.awaitDirective(directivePath, {... signal: deps.signal})`. This poll loop is where the founder-stop artifact must be detected before any dispatch/nudge.
- The next-directive dispatch (a nudge) is sent at the bottom of the loop (~1556): `oscarDriver.send(buildNextOrWrapDispatch(...))`. After an atom verifies+commits, control returns here.
- Contract module already landed in atom 0: packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts exposes `readFounderStopSignal(runDir)`, `writeResumeState`, `resumeStatePath`, and the `PreDispatchResumeState` type. Reuse them; do not redefine.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add a `holdRun(park)` function in runner.ts, sibling to `stopRun()`, that performs a NON-terminal halt: it must NOT kill bob's work for quarantine, MUST NOT call setWorkItemStatus(...,'abandoned'), MUST NOT call quarantineAtom; it persists the resume-state via writeResumeState(runDir, park), records a new `run-held` store event, sets RunStatus `held` (added in atom 0), writes the run record, and returns a RunResult with status `held`. Panes stay open (do not tear down). Any already-made commits remain on the branch.
2. Introduce a file-triggered halt signal distinct from StopRequestedError: e.g. a `FounderHeldError` carrying the `PreDispatchResumeState` park payload (put it in founder-stop.ts next to the existing error/types). At the directive boundary, poll `readFounderStopSignal(runDir)` inside the same await/poll cycle as `io.awaitDirective` (so it halts PROMPTLY without waiting for a directive); when present, throw FounderHeldError with a `pre-dispatch` park record { park:'pre-dispatch', atomNumber:n, directive: <the next directive if one is already on disk, else the standing parked directive> }. If no directive is on disk yet, park atomNumber=n with the directive that WOULD be dispatched is not yet known — in that case persist just the atom boundary; do NOT fabricate a directive. (Keep the park record valid against the atom-0 schema; if a pre-dispatch park with no directive isn't representable, extend the atom-0 type minimally and update its test rather than writing an invalid record.)
3. Catch FounderHeldError at the outer try/catch (the same place that handles isStopRequestedError) and `return await holdRun(park)` — BEFORE the StopRequestedError check is fine, but the two must stay independent: AbortSignal still routes to stopRun()/`stopped`; the file artifact routes to holdRun()/`held`. `held` must be reachable ONLY through the file artifact (ADR-0037).
4. Commit-boundary race: after an atom verifies `pass` and commits (the commit happens inside executeAgentStep), control returns to the bottom of the loop. BEFORE sending `buildNextOrWrapDispatch` (the next-directive nudge), poll the artifact; if a founder stop is registered, do NOT send the dispatch — park `pre-dispatch` at the next atom boundary and hold. This guarantees a verified atom is committed while the halt parks cleanly at the boundary with no further dispatch.

FOUNDER-EXPLICIT-ONLY: the runner never synthesizes the founder-stop artifact; it only ever READS it. There is no self-stop code path. Keep that invariant.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (add to packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts or a new tests/founder-stop-hold.test.ts, using the existing runner test harness/fakes):
- A runner test proving: with a founder-stop artifact present at the directive boundary, the loop emits NO further dispatch to Oscar and NO nudge, calls neither setWorkItemStatus('abandoned') nor quarantineAtom, and the run settles to status `held` with a `run-held` event and a persisted resume-state file.
- A test of the commit-boundary race: an atom that verifies `pass` commits, THEN (artifact now present) the run holds at `held` without sending the next-directive dispatch; the verified atom's commit is intact.
- A test proving the two lanes stay separate: AbortSignal/StopRequestedError still produces terminal `stopped` (abandoned+quarantined) — `held` is NOT reachable via the signal, only via the artifact.
- Exit criterion: `cd '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder/packages/core' && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test` GREEN (paste the tail + exit code, and the names of the new hold tests).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- stopRun(), teardownRun(), POST /runs/:id/stop, the AbortSignal path, quarantine, and all existing runner tests stay green and unchanged in behavior.
- Do NOT wire the during-exec or pre-verdict boundaries (inside executeAgentStep) — that is the next atom. Do not close ticket 0031 yet.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts, packages/core/tests/** (runner.test.ts and/or a new founder-stop-hold test; and founder-stop.test.ts only if you minimally extend the atom-0 park type). One-shot, judgment-gated; not loop-amenable.

data.reason: Phase-1 runtime part A correct: holdRun() halts non-terminally (status held, no abandon/no quarantine, resume-state persisted, run-held event, panes open); directive-boundary poll + FounderHeldError route only the file artifact to held while AbortSignal stays terminal stopped (lanes proven separate); commit-boundary race holds after a verified commit without sending NEXT. tsc clean, full core suite 497/497 green with 3 substantive new tests asserting no-dispatch/no-nudge, the commit-race, and lane separation. during-exec/pre-verdict correctly deferred; ticket 0031 not yet closed.

awaiting-directive atom 2: awaiting Oscar's directive for atom 2

data.task:
Phase 1 runtime, part B for ADR-0037 — wire the remaining two founder-stop boundaries INSIDE executeAgentStep so a founder halt during Bob execution or during verify parks resume-ready into `held`, completing the ADR's three-point resume-state contract. This builds directly on atom 1's holdRun()/FounderHeldError. Do NOT close ticket 0031 yet (that is the next atom).

OWNER MAP (read before changing):
- packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts: executeAgentStep. The DURING-EXEC boundary is the `for(;;)` monitor loop (~146-217) whose `runMonitor(...)` call blocks until the builder is done/dead/timeout — so detecting the artifact only between monitor runs is NOT prompt; you must race it. The PRE-VERDICT boundary is `awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog('verify', atomIndex, ..., () => io.awaitVerification(verifyPath, {...signal}))` (~237-238). Its surrounding `catch (err)` (~242) currently does `if (isStopRequestedError(err)) throw err; setWorkItemStatus(abandoned); fail('verify-failed')` — a FounderHeldError must be RE-THROWN there BEFORE the abandon/fail, exactly like StopRequestedError.
- packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts: holdRun(park) (atom 1) and the outer catch `if (isFounderHeldError(err)) return await holdRun(err.park)`. The nudge-watchdog `judge` (~1166) already returns done when `stage === 'directive'` and the artifact is present — broaden that check to fire for ANY stage (so verify nudges also cease on a registered stop).
- packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts: FounderHeldError.park is typed PreDispatchResumeState (atom 1). Broaden it to the full `ResumeState` union; the DuringExecResumeState and PreVerdictResumeState types already exist (atom 0). isFounderHeldError unchanged.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. founder-stop.ts: change FounderHeldError to carry `readonly park: ResumeState` (the union), not just PreDispatchResumeState.
2. runner.ts holdRun(park: ResumeState): generalize it to accept the union. Derive the atom count and the `run-held` event payload from the park regardless of variant (pre-dispatch uses `atomNumber`; during-exec/pre-verdict use `activeAtomNumber`). Add a tiny helper for the park's atom number; keep the existing non-terminal behavior (no kill of Bob, NO setWorkItemStatus('abandoned'), NO quarantineAtom, status `held`, writeResumeState, run-held event, panes open). The during-exec/pre-verdict held atom's work item stays `open` (parked, not abandoned).
3. agent-step.ts DURING-EXEC: race the `runMonitor(...)` await against a founder-stop watcher that polls `readFounderStopSignal(runDir)` on `t.pollMs` and throws `FounderHeldError` with a during-exec park `{ park:'during-exec', activeAtomNumber: atomIndex, directive, waitMonitorCursor }` once the artifact appears (reuse atom 1's race shape from awaitDirectiveOrFounderHold — a settled-flag + Promise.race). `waitMonitorCursor` must be a REAL re-attach handle (not an empty object): include at least the builder session ref (bobDriver.refId), the live monitor position (monitorSamples and completionAttempt), and the active doneSentinel — enough that Phase 2 can re-attach to Bob's in-flight work or re-dispatch the same directive. Do NOT kill Bob and do NOT mark the work item abandoned on hold.
4. agent-step.ts PRE-VERDICT: race the verify await the same way (or poll inside the same await) and throw `FounderHeldError` with `{ park:'pre-verdict', activeAtomNumber: atomIndex, verifyRequest }` where verifyRequest captures the request already handed to Oscar: at least `{ verifyPath, directivePath, atom: atomIndex }` — enough to re-issue the SAME verify with no commit and no atom allocation. In the verify `catch`, add `if (isFounderHeldError(err)) throw err` before the StopRequested check / abandon / fail.
5. Ensure FounderHeldError thrown from executeAgentStep propagates cleanly to runner.ts's outer catch → holdRun (it already will, since executeAgentStep is called inside the loop's try); just make sure no intermediate catch in agent-step.ts swallows or mistreats it (the verify catch is the only one — fixed in step 4).

FOUNDER-EXPLICIT-ONLY stays intact: the runner only ever READS the artifact; no self-stop path.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (add to packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts using the existing harness; the founder-stop signal helper `writeFounderStopSignal` already exists there from atom 1):
- DURING-EXEC test: with a founder-stop artifact appearing while Bob is being monitored (e.g. write it from the fake builder's readScreen/monitor hook, or pre-write so the first monitor sample sees it), the run settles to `held`, atoms reflects the active atom, NO verify dispatch is sent, NO further nudge, the work item is NOT abandoned and NOT quarantined (restoreToHead never called), a `run-held` event with a `during-exec` park is recorded, and the persisted resume-state is a during-exec record whose waitMonitorCursor carries the builder ref (non-empty).
- PRE-VERDICT test: with the artifact appearing while awaiting the verify verdict (write it instead of producing a verdict), the run settles to `held`, NO commit happens (no addAndCommit for the atom), the work item is NOT abandoned and NOT quarantined, a `run-held` event with a `pre-verdict` park is recorded, and the persisted resume-state is a pre-verdict record carrying the verifyPath.
- Regression: the existing abort-while-monitoring test still yields terminal `stopped` with abandon+quarantine (lanes stay separate); all prior founder-stop (directive-boundary, commit-boundary) tests stay green.
- Exit criterion: `cd '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder/packages/core' && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test` GREEN (paste the tail + exit code and the new test names).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- stopRun()/teardown/AbortSignal path/quarantine and all existing tests stay green and behaviorally unchanged. held remains reachable ONLY via the file artifact.
- Do not close ticket 0031, do not edit Plays/docs, do not touch packages outside core.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts, packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts, packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts. One-shot, judgment-gated; not loop-amenable.

data.reason: Phase-1 runtime part B completes the three-point resume-state contract: during-exec and pre-verdict boundaries in executeAgentStep race the founder-stop artifact and throw FounderHeldError (broadened to the ResumeState union); holdRun parks non-terminally (work item stays open, no abandon/quarantine, Bob not killed) and persists during-exec (directive + builderRef cursor) / pre-verdict (verify request) records; verify catch re-throws past abandon; monitor-abort cleanly stops the poll while preserving the AbortSignal->stopped lane. tsc clean, full core suite 499/499 with 2 substantive new tests; ticket 0031 correctly left for the next atom.

data.task:
Phase 1 landing closeout for ADR-0037 — two parts: (A) pin the last unpinned Phase-1 verification bullet with a test, then (B) close ticket 0031 via the live ticket-close convention. Phase-1 behavior already landed across commits 3308873 (held disposition + contract), c9f7a3e (directive boundary + holdRun), and 6d39ecd (during-exec + pre-verdict boundaries).

PART A — founder-explicit-only test (no persona self-stop path; ADR-0037 'Verified when' bullet 2).
The invariant: a founder stop is reachable ONLY through an externally-written founder-stop artifact recording an explicit founder direction; the runner/runtime never synthesizes it and there is no programmatic self-stop. Pin it in packages/core/tests with BOTH:
  1. A behavioral assertion: a normal full run that NEVER writes a founder-stop artifact settles to its ordinary terminal status (completed/wrapped — NOT 'held'), records NO 'run-held' event, and leaves NO founder-stop signal file in the run dir (assert via existsSync(founderStopSignalPath(runDir)) === false). Reuse the existing runner.test.ts harness and the founderStopSignalPath import already present.
  2. A module-surface assertion (in founder-stop.test.ts or runner.test.ts): packages/core/src/runner/founder-stop.ts exposes the READER readFounderStopSignal and the resume-state writer writeResumeState, but NO exported function that writes/creates the founder-stop SIGNAL artifact — i.e. the only producer of founder-stop.json is an external persona recording founder direction, never runner code. Assert the module's exported surface contains no signal-writer (e.g. assert there is no export whose name writes the stop signal, and that STOP_SIGNAL_FILENAME has no companion write helper).

PART B — close ticket 0031 following cocoder/tickets/AGENTS.md §Lifecycle exactly:
  - The ticket file is cocoder/tickets/open/0031-founder-stop-the-run-control-for-personas.md. In its frontmatter set `status: Closed`, add `closed: 2026-06-23`, and add a `resolution:` line. MOVE the file to cocoder/tickets/closed/0031-founder-stop-the-run-control-for-personas.md (create at the closed path, remove from open/).
  - Resolution text (concise, conclusion-first): Phase 1 of ADR-0037 landed — a founder 'stop the run' direction given to any persona, recorded as a file-based founder-stop artifact, halts the runner loop at the directive, during-exec, or verify boundary and parks the run resume-ready in the new non-terminal `held` disposition (panes open; the in-flight atom is NOT abandoned or quarantined); held is reachable only via the founder artifact and stays separate from the terminal AbortSignal->stopped lane and from teardown. Founder-explicit-only is enforced and tested. Cite commits 3308873, c9f7a3e, 6d39ecd. Note Phase 2 (resume from held) remains open under ADR-0037 and is NOT closed by this ticket.
  - Update cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md (SSOT mirror): remove the 0031 row from the Open table and add it to the Recently Closed table with Closed=2026-06-23 and a one-line resolution. Keep the table shapes intact; touch only the 0031 rows.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `cd '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder/packages/core' && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test` GREEN (paste tail + exit + the new test name(s)).
- Run any governance/SSOT suite that asserts ticket INDEX consistency: first `grep -rl "0031\|tickets/INDEX\|tickets/open\|tickets/closed" packages/*/tests packages/*/src` to find tests that pin ticket state; run the package suite(s) that match and confirm GREEN (paste evidence). If nothing references ticket 0031 or the INDEX structure, state that explicitly with the grep output.
- Confirm by `ls` that 0031 now exists under cocoder/tickets/closed/ and NOT under cocoder/tickets/open/, and that INDEX.md shows it under Recently Closed (paste the two rows).

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK:
- Do not weaken or delete any existing founder-stop test. Do not touch the runtime behavior landed in atoms 0-2. Do not reopen or alter any other ticket or INDEX row. Do not edit the ADR.
- Keep the full core suite green, not just the new test.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/tests/** (runner.test.ts and/or founder-stop.test.ts), cocoder/tickets/open/0031-founder-stop-the-run-control-for-personas.md (remove after move), cocoder/tickets/closed/0031-founder-stop-the-run-control-for-personas.md (new), cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md. One-shot, judgment-gated; not loop-amenable.
